title: T-002 — oee_snapshots site_id extension + read-only consumer indexes (OEE §9.1, REC-L1)
prompt:
# T-002 — oee_snapshots site_id extension

PRD: docs/prd/15-OEE-PRD.md §9.1, §6 (REC-L1 site_id / D-OEE-1 consumer contract), §14.5 (data residency)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Apply the OEE-scoped extension to the `oee_snapshots` table owned by 08-PRODUCTION (PRD §9.1 verbatim): add nullable `site_id UUID` REC-L1 column + `idx_oee_site_line_time(site_id, line_id, snapshot_minute DESC)` index. Verify RLS reuses the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function. 15-OEE is a READ-ONLY consumer of this table — no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE paths added here.

## Pre-flight schema check
Before drafting, read `packages/db/migrations/` to verify: (a) latest migration number, (b) `oee_snapshots` already exists from 08-PRODUCTION §9.9 (skip table creation if so; this task is ALTER only), (c) `app.current_org_id()` function present (Wave1 T-125), (d) `sites` and `site_user_access` tables exist for FK (14-multi-site; if absent, `site_id` is plain UUID NULL with comment noting the constraint is added by 14-multi-site).

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/NNNN_oee_a_snapshots_site_id.sql` (NNNN = next free): `ALTER TABLE oee_snapshots ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS site_id UUID;` and `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_oee_site_line_time ON oee_snapshots(site_id, line_id, snapshot_minute DESC);`.
2. If RLS policies on oee_snapshots are absent or reference `current_setting()`, this task adds/replaces them to reference `app.current_org_id()` and a site_id filter via `site_user_access`. If policies already correct, no-op (idempotent migration).
3. Drizzle schema entry `packages/db/src/schema/oee/snapshots.ts` (read-only typed `oeeSnapshots` table mirroring 08-PRODUCTION definition + new site_id col). Export typed Select only (no Insert from 15-OEE).
4. Vitest covering: (a) column exists post-migration, (b) RLS isolation across two orgs using `app.set_org_context`, (c) policy text references `app.current_org_id()` not `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')`, (d) EXPLAIN of `SELECT ... WHERE site_id=? AND line_id=? ORDER BY snapshot_minute DESC LIMIT 60` uses `idx_oee_site_line_time`.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/NNNN_oee_a_snapshots_site_id.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/oee/snapshots.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/oee-a-snapshots-site-id.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs on a DB where oee_snapshots exists, when `\d oee_snapshots` is read, then site_id UUID NULL column and idx_oee_site_line_time index are present and RLS+FORCE RLS remain enabled.
2. Given two orgs and `app.set_org_context(token, orgA)` / `(token, orgB)`, when `SELECT * FROM oee_snapshots` runs as `app_user` in each session, then each only sees its org's rows.
3. Given `pg_policies` is inspected, when policies for oee_snapshots are listed, then USING/WITH CHECK reference `app.current_org_id()` and contain no `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC reads.
4. Given `EXPLAIN SELECT ... WHERE site_id=$1 AND line_id=$2 ORDER BY snapshot_minute DESC LIMIT 60`, when run, then the planner uses idx_oee_site_line_time.

## Test strategy
- RED: write `packages/db/__tests__/oee-a-snapshots-site-id.test.ts` asserting four ACs before migration exists.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test oee-a-snapshots-site-id`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not write INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE paths against oee_snapshots from 15-OEE; the table is owned by 08-PRODUCTION (D-OEE-1).
- Do not name the business-scope column tenant_id; Wave0 v4.3 lock is org_id.
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC; use foundation `app.current_org_id()`.
- Do not skip ENABLE+FORCE RLS or omit policies.
- Do not retrofit site_id later — it is REC-L1 day-1 per §6.3.

pipeline_inputs.description: ALTER oee_snapshots to add site_id REC-L1 column + idx_oee_site_line_time index; verify RLS uses app.current_org_id(); read-only Drizzle schema for 15-OEE consumer surface.
pipeline_inputs.details: PRD anchor corrected from §6.3 to §6 by Fixer F12 2026-05-14 (anchor sweep; §6.3 does not exist in 15-OEE — §6 Decisions D-OEE-1..7 covers the consumer contract including D-OEE-1 oee_snapshots ownership). Closes Auditor A OEE-001 prerequisite. 15-OEE consumes oee_snapshots written by 08-PRODUCTION per D-OEE-1; this task adds the OEE-scoped REC-L1 site_id day-1 and ensures the consumer query pattern (per-line 24h trend, per-shift heatmap) has the supporting composite index. Cross-module: 08-PRODUCTION owns the producer (`oee_snapshots` table created in its migration). If the table is not yet present in the migrations history, this task fails fast with a clear error pointing at the missing 08-PRODUCTION migration — it does NOT create the table.
out_of_scope: Do not create oee_snapshots table (08-PRODUCTION owns)
Do not create MVs here (T-006, T-007)
acceptance_criteria: Given migration runs on a DB where oee_snapshots exists, when \d oee_snapshots is read, then site_id UUID NULL + idx_oee_site_line_time index are present and RLS+FORCE RLS remain enabled

Given pg_policies is inspected, when policies are listed, then USING/WITH CHECK reference app.current_org_id() with no current_setting() GUC reads
Given EXPLAIN of the (site_id, line_id, snapshot_minute DESC) read query, when run, then idx_oee_site_line_time is used

Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test oee-a-snapshots-site-id`

Do not skip ENABLE+FORCE RLS or omit policies
